title: normalize_base_url is duplicated inline in providers/mod.rs::fetch_models
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Architectural Alignment / DRY

Location: src/proxy.rs line 40-46 (fn normalize_base_url) AND src/providers/mod.rs lines 163-167 (inline duplicate inside fetch_models).

Violation: normalize_base_url is a public free function in proxy.rs with docstring explaining its purpose (strip trailing slash and /v1). In providers/mod.rs::fetch_models, the SAME logic is inlined:
  let normalized_base = base_url
      .trim_end_matches('/')
      .trim_end_matches("/v1")
      .trim_end_matches('/');
This is a 3-line copy-paste. fetch_models does not import normalize_base_url. Any fix (e.g., also stripping /v1beta for Gemini) must be applied in both places.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. Move normalize_base_url into src/providers/mod.rs since it's a cross-cutting utility used by both provider-side URL construction (fetch_models) and proxy-side forwarding. Or put it in a shared src/url_utils.rs.
  2. Delete the inline version at mod.rs lines 163-167; call `normalize_base_url(base_url)`.
  3. Consider also stripping `/v1beta` for Gemini — but only after confirming Google's fetch_models endpoint works with the stripped form.
  4. Add a unit test: `assert_eq!(normalize_base_url("https://api.example.com/v1/"), "https://api.example.com")`.
